mission.title: Install and Configure Openkruise on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenKruise is a CNCF incubating project that automates the management of large-scale applications on Kubernetes. It provides advanced workload management capabilities, making it easier to handle stateless, stateful, and job applications efficiently.

steps.title: Add the OpenKruise Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenKruise Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kruise https://charts.kruise.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenKruise using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenKruise in a new namespace called 'kruise-system' with the following command:
```bash
helm install kruise kruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --create-namespace --version v1.8.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenKruise pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kruise-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for OpenKruise
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kruise-controller-manager -n kruise-system
```
Add the following under the 'spec.template.spec.containers' section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for OpenKruise
steps.description:
Apply the necessary RBAC permissions for OpenKruise with the following command:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openkruise/kruise/master/deploy/rbac.yaml
```

resolution.summary: After completing these steps, you should have OpenKruise installed and running in your Kubernetes cluster. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods in the 'kruise-system' namespace.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kruise https://charts.kruise.io
helm repo update
helm install kruise kruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --create-namespace --version v1.8.3

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
